name: Sponge Cake (Pan Di Spagna)
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter and line a 9- or 10-inch round cake pan that is 2 inches deep with parchment paper.
2. In a medium mixing bowl, whisk the yolks with the vanilla extract.
3. Whisk in half the sugar and continue to beat until very light and frothy, about 5 minutes, using a hand mixer set at medium speed or a heavy-duty mixer fitted with the whip.
4. Combine the flour and cornstarch and sift once to aerate.
5. In a clean, dry bowl, beat the egg whites with salt until they hold a soft peak using a hand mixer set at medium speed or a heavy-duty mixer fitted with the whip. Beat in the remaining sugar in a slow stream until the egg whites hold a firm peak.
6. Fold the yolk mixture into the whites with a rubber spatula.
7. Sift the flour and cornstarch over the eggs in three additions, folding them in gradually. Do not overmix the batter.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
9. Bake at 350°F for 30 to 40 minutes, until well risen and firm when gently pressed with the palm of your hand. Loosen the layer from the side of the pan with a small knife or spatula, invert onto a rack, and leave the paper attached.
10. Turn the layer right side up and cool on a rack. Store tightly wrapped in plastic in the refrigerator for up to 5 days, or freeze.
